channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: SBI partners with Solana Foundation — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SBI partners with Solana Foundation

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:17 UTC

OP

>Japanese financial giant SBI partners with Solana Foundation to build on-chain financial market in Japan
LMAOOOO, XRP BAGGIES ON SUICIDE WATCH

Early replies (first 3)

62475088
>>62475081
While ripple partners with nigger ball teams, their best “partners” build real shit on other chains, kek

62475247
>>62475081
SBI is probably Ripple's biggest claim to fame, yet look at this shit.

62476546
>>62475247
Yeah, they’ve been talking about how SBI and Ripple are best buddies and how they will run everything on XRPL for years, but look at that. Failure after failure, kek.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476565
>>62475081
The interconnected multi chain future is being build

62476573
>>62475081
Linkie cope thread. They already use XRP in multiple of their divisions and have an XRP subsection in every shareholder report. They are Ripple's largest outside shareholder and formed a joint company with them for remittances in Asia called SBI Ripple Asia and give their tradfi depositors XRP incentives with Kitao regularly talking about XRP in shareholder meetings and thei CEO of Global Asset Management saying that "XRP is not just another crypto, this is the wealth transfer of a generation" (inb4 corny deliberate misreading characterizing this as the sort of extractive wealth transfer from bagholders that link is when that is clearly not what he meant). They issued their NFTs on the XRPL, partnered with arab royalty (both of which have execs transferring to management) on Vivo Power to drive XRP adoption etc. Pretty sure we're good as SBI is concerned. Lol
>>62475247
It isn't even close to their biggest claim to fame and Ripple themselves partner with CLL for price feeds for their ETH RLUSD product (which is the minor chain issued onto with most on the XRPL). Neither partnership means fucking anything for the price of the link token or link token holders which is why you are mad and made this post to cope. Lol

62476715
>>62476573
>formed a joint company with them for remittances in Asia called SBI Ripple Asia
... which is itself a massive failure.

No wonder they're moving onto Solana and Chainlink.

62476771
As part of this partnership, the Solana Foundation will join SBI R3 Japan Co., Ltd. (Headquarters: Minato-ku, Tokyo; President and CEO: Mamoru Fujimoto; to be renamed "SBI Solana Global Co., Ltd." in the future) and, together with its shareholders SBI Holdings and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "SMFG"), will promote a new growth strategy.

https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/news/2026/0713_16478.html

SBI has a long-standing strategic relationship with Ripple that predates the R3 JV: SBI invested in Ripple and created joint ventures such as SBI Ripple Asia to promote XRP-powered payments in Japan and the region, and SBI participated in cross-border payments trials with R3 Consortium members.

https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/news/pdf/2016/0719_b_en.pdf

The irony of the fudcuck post is that the chainlink partnership enables ripple and solana. So far no entity took a sizeable stake in the chainlink token.

62476808
>>62476715
I knew you would post the same photo with the same brown hands. You don't even know how to spell remittances. Its remittance arm, SBI Remit operate the largest volume ODL corridor in the world. It has processed a cumulative international volume of more than ¥2.5 trillion (approximately $15+ billion). It took SBI Remit 14 years to hit its first ¥2 trillion in volume but it accumulated its next ¥500 billion ($3.2 billion USD) in just 17 months as corridors rapidly scaled showing it is clearly growing in a non-linear clip as a business. You are posting a picture you are mischaracterizing the quote of from years ago. Today they connect over 100 institutions across 55+ countries. Further, Lol XRP (intentionally by design) doesn't have smart contracts, you fucking retard. Meanwhile:
https://ripple.com/ripple-press/ripple-and-sbi-group-partner-to-launch-ripple-usd-in-japan/

https://www.fintechobserver.com/sbi-ripple-asia-secures-regulatory-approval-for-xrp-ledger-based-prepaid-token-platform/

62476815
>>62476771
This.

62476862
>>62476808
>You are posting a picture you are mischaracterizing the quote of from years ago
His quote is from a video I once linked him (biepum). He took the first snippet of how they started and what the hard parts were when building (Banks make money off the "friction " of cross border payments, so they are not keen on throwing that profitable part out. Same as with the yield on stablecoins in USA). He loves to frame everything out of context and start the argument that way. Very jewish/jeetlike behavior. This type of discussion is literally all he has in life so go easy on him.

62476914
>verbo pretending to be two people again

62476941
>>62476914
Biepum projecting again. Debate the person, not the matter , oi vey.

62476967
>>62476941
There's nothing to debate.
Even Ripple's most loyal partners and literal shareholders are going multichain.

62477005
>>62476967
You act as if "the future is multichain" is some kind of gotcha.

You came in to fud with out of context garbage (modus operandi of biepum the linktard) and are already resorting in weasling out by making a vague non argument statement.

Post the collage you fudcuck. I know you have it.

62477013
>>62477005
>You act as if "the future is multichain" is some kind of gotcha.
it is if your whole premise for existing is "everyone will use us and nothing else".

62477051
>>62477013
>co create the INTERledger protocol
>fudcuck spins it to they only want 1 ledger to be used

62477074
>>62477051
bro you're on soundcloud? What's your @ unironically
Autists like you make the best bleeps and bloops.

62477173
>>62477074
>please dox yourself so i can make another collage

62477391
>>62476862
100% though I did not know the origin of his getting it. He used to do the exact same thing literally "very fine people" style with David's tweet about the value of XRP which literally had a second tweet in thread that explicitly proved him wrong and so many disingenuous faggots like him tried to selectively clip to fud David did a longform address to the claim outright contradicting it.

62477396
>>62476914
I'm not samefagging you fucking NPC. I literally never samefag pretending to be anyone but me.

62477418
>>62476967
Are you really so low IQ you would imagine that banks and financial institutions would confine their total business to not include smart contracts just to keep the pie chart of chains used all one color? Do you think they would similarly silo themselves to one chain's liquidity for same reason? Do you similarly believe Ripple and the XRPL would be increased by them doing this and not have dramatic reduction in pie radii by having such services ringfenced from them and given to competitors, remembering that XRP's whole core thesis is building out the interconnective cross-network liquidity/value layer? Are you really this stupid, do you not know this basic 101 level detail about or are you just coping by being disingenuous?

item.title: isnt this kinda sussy baka — 35 replies — 67.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
isnt this kinda sussy baka

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-07-13 19:49 UTC

OP

why is btc have value if xmr is needed to purchase things on dark web but btc is just a coin and is not needed. xmr is needed and therefore has more value, right? forgive me if im wrong but, is the reason BTC is still alive just because it's the one that got all the attention at the start?

also anons check with ur library to see if theres a morningstar dashboard, might be nice if there is.

btw has anyone here looked into cs2 markets or anything? i know it sounds crazy, but people think crypto is crazy too.

Early replies (first 3)

62468595
>>62468583
Xmr is for midwits to hold and high IQ individuals to use like the utility it is. You wouldn't understand my simpleton friend

62468770
>>62468583
My friend makes money on the side with CS2 market flips. Waits for update news, buys up shit that's cheap but won't be because of some bullshit changes, then just waits. Chinks pump up the prices like crazy and he benefits.

62468973
>>62468583
Because bitcoin's price is based on speculation
if it's price was based on use it wouldn't even be a 100 dollars

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62470399
>>62468583
U sound like a pedo. Pretty much anyone who supports xmr is a pedo

62470417
>>62470399
I'm sure they use it, but aren't most of the transactions from drug distributors and users?

62471297
>>62468583
Because institutions are all-in on btc. Why not XMR? Because Monero doesn't work in the system's favor. The elites don't need to care if all of their transactions are public, they are just using shadow corporations.
So in a sense btc became a stand-in for all cryptos in the minds of normies.

>>62470104
There would literally be no demand for XMR if it primarily was an sov.

62471322
>>62470104
>If Bitcoin was able to get to 126k with just whale collusion and bullshit
It only got to that price because of institutionalization and trump pumping it just to dump. Without that it hit 1/2 that price. XMR isn't even readily available to ameritards due to it's standing. Yet, BTC was always available.
Holy cope of a thread. FPBP

62471336
>>62468595
This. So normies need a larger more robust coin for privacy to lean on. Similar to other markets leaning on property and gold to justify their own worth during crisis.

62471599
>>62468583
xmr will eventually reach five figures by becoming a significant beneficiary of capital flight from the horrors of late-stage statism

62472716
>>62471297
>the law of supply and demand doesn’t apply to Monero if people like it and want to hold it
I’m just so tired of shills/retards/BTC-brainbroken people applying new rules just for Monero. What does it even mean for a coin to be “primarily a SoV”? People use Monero for whatever reason they want. Some use it primarily for transacting (because it works) and some use it primarily for holding (because it works). That’s how a fucking functional currency operates.

>>62471322
>a shitcoin with literally no utility outside of a 21M collection size NFT was available to buy on Coinbase
>this is relevant to Monero
At the end of the day it comes down to whether you actually believe crypto is a real technology or not. Is it possible to create a censorship resistant asset with privacy that actually works as the native internet currency of the world? Because Monero is LITERALLY the only one that even has a chance of becoming that—-the entire point of crypto in the first place. No amount of regulation or liquidity restriction can prevent it from taking hold eventually. The world is far too multipolar now, no single nation can stop it.

62472732
>>62468595
>Makes fun of people who think that holding XMR will achieve anything except making it a new object to gamble on

>>62470104
>Gets mad about positive price development coming from the usage of XMR and not just stacking it because you can't force demand by choking supply

>>62471297
>Explains that even if just over 50% of XMR is used for holding demand would fall rapidly

>>62472716
>Doesn't understand a thing but assumes this is because of the psyop that is modern BTC usage despite it being mainly valued because of it being an sov and institutionalized

62472733
>hasanabi thread full of faggots sucking cock and screaming at each other
You losers are exactly what everyonr thinks of you lmfaooooo

62472759
>>62472732
>supply and demand doesn’t apply to Monero
You can keep pretending this is the case, but it isn’t.

62472781
>>62472759
>Bro people totally want a currency that no one uses

62472786
>>62472732
>bitcoin is valuable because it’s a sov
>if you think Monero would go up if it became more of a sov you’re retarded

>bitcoin has no usecase but it’s valuable people people hold it
>if even 50% of XMR was held, demand would fall rapidly
You’re just a transparent shill faggot. You have no basis for any of this. Why would demand fall when the supply shrinks? Literally economics 101: supply shrinks, price RISES. But the rules change when you replace “BTC” with “XMR”. You’re FULL OF SHIT

62472793
>>62472786
>Guys lets just copy the psyop that has been adopted by all the jewish system
Man, I really overlooked that possibility didn't I?

62472795
>>62468583
There's this thing called speculation, go learn about it.
It pretty much sums up all crypto.

62472796
>>62472793
>holding a coin is Jewish
Literally what the FUCK are you taking about

62472807
>>62472796
>BTC totally hasn't been integrated into the jewish system
Anon, you've already lost. Come back in a month telling us we need more compliance so judenstein can buy gorillions of XMR

62472813
>>62472807
Buy Monero. Hold Monero. That’s all I, OP, or anyone else here says.

You can pretend that means “compliance” but it doesn’t, and it’s telling that you have to blatantly lie just to make your point.

62472830
>>62472813
Again, why would anyone want to get into Monero if all it does is being held? There's no reason. The value of XMR stems from its transactional qualities. Why would I buy your bags? What would you even do with them once you're ready to transact your XMR? Do you just want to give some illiquid good to your kids when you kick the bucket?
>But it works for BTC
Because it's been psyoped and adopted by the jews for that reason

62472843
>>62472830
You’re just flailing around autistically screeching right now. It’s sad. You don’t have to be this way.

62473043
>>62472843
Please tell me why anyone would buy XMR if it's primarily just moonfag's bags

62473108
>>62473043
moe and sov aren't mutually exclusive, false dichotomy

62473123
>>62473108
>There would literally be no demand for XMR if it primarily was an sov. (>>62471297)

Try to keep up

62473376
>>62473123
>see? I said some bullshit before. No I don’t have to prove it. I just said it.

62473396
>>62473376
I already established that if XMR was primarily used as an sov it's demand would drop massively. Fomo only works if the product is attractive due to internal motivation

62473588
>>62473396
What the fuck do you mean you “established it”? You literally just asserted it without the slightest attempt at supporting your argument.

“Monero demand would not drop massively if people held it. In fact that claim violates the most basic principles of economics”

There. I established the counterpoint. What now?
>(am I actually just arguing with an autistic teenager? It seems like it)

62473623
>>62473588
>What the fuck do you mean you “established it”? You literally just asserted it without the slightest attempt at supporting your argument.
The word 'if' signals that an argument makes an assumption. Any discussions derived from that argument which are not attacking the assumption itself presuppose the assumption.
>There. I established the counterpoint.
Your point isn't countering mine. That's because I used the word 'primarily'. This means the assumption of my argument is that over 50% of Monero's 'usage' was solely as an sov. Your point is about any non-zero amount of XMR as an sov.
>In fact that claim violates the most basic principles of economics”
This is wrong. There is simply no reason to buy your bags. If Monero was primarily used as an sov there would be no meaningful difference to a non-KYCed btc wallet. You could argue about fungibility now but not even half the board knows this term so this is a negligible factor.
I also told you before that even if you are about to argue that it worked out for btc you're neglecting
A. btc has now been adopted by the jewish system, Monero is too democratic in its power concentration to have the same happening to it
B. XMR's qualities stem from its transactional qualities so with every hodler added the appeal of XMR shrinks
So if you want to defend your position you now need to show us why people would be interested into entering XMR if it was *primarily* used as an sov. No one is arguing about supply and demand not existing as a framework for finding the value of something, we're arguing about demand existing in actuality under the assumption of XMR mainly being used as an sov.

62474147
>>62473623
>Any discussions derived from that argument which are not attacking the assumption itself presuppose the assumption.
This statement alone should be enough to stop anyone from ever talking to you again.

62474174
>>62468583
correct. xmr is the real bitcoin because it has all the satoshi requirements

62474407
>>62473623
>I used the word 'primarily'
So? This whole idea that Monero is “primarily” any particular thing already presupposes your bullshit idea that MoE and SoV are inherently contradictory or adversarial with one another. They’re not.

Monero is not primarily anything. It’s not primarily transactional it’s not primarily store of value. It is whatever people want it to be.

>There is simply no reason to buy your bags.
lol ok weirdo I’m gonna just buy and hold Monero and nobody will ever be able to detect it, tax it, freeze it, trace it, or seize it. But apparently that’s a worthless utility.

>XMR's qualities stem from its transactional qualities
Repeating this doesn’t make it true btw.

62477241
>>62474147
Sadly it doesn't.

>>62474407
>This whole idea that Monero is “primarily” any particular thing already presupposes your bullshit idea that MoE and SoV are inherently contradictory or adversarial with one another.
No, I said that because you complained in >>62470104 about different people using XMR differently.
>They’re not.
The value proposition of XMR as an sov derives solely from its qualities of it being an moe. So by you arguing for sov, you're reducing its sov properties.
>It is whatever people want it to be.
But then he cries about >>62468595
>But apparently that’s a worthless utility.
It's only not worthless because people actually use it.
>Repeating this doesn’t make it true btw.
It being true is what makes it true.

item.title: >mfw it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen The world economy is literally… — 24 replies — 54.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>mfw it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen The world economy is literally…

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-13 19:53 UTC

OP

>mfw it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen

The world economy is literally rigged

Early replies (first 3)

62470620
most US citizens don't make that much either

62470627
>>62470461
Come to America. Join us. Vote Republican. Embrace classical liberal Americana. Hate everything communist and fascist. You will thrive.

62470632
>>62470627
ok give me a visa
i would leave europe tomorrow

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62470641
>>62470632
Actual American here. Sorry we’re full unfortunately!

62470644
>>62470632
Only jeets allowed

62470648
>>62470632
We'll trade you for 10 Haitians.

>>62470641
No, we need to trade. Let the loving communist Europeans take our "refugees".

62470923
>>62470620
Yes, but I am highly qualified and I make 15k a year in a third world country. If I were american i would earn at least 90k a year

62471418
>>62470461
Half of that 100k, if you even make that much (few do), goes towards healthcare.

62471423
>>62470461
pretty sure we got illegal immigrants working construction making that. The secret is to be in the US, not necessarily to be a citizen

62471442
>>62470632
>public transit, subsidized healthcare, labor and consumer protections, social safety net, basic human rights that still carry weight, and mostly functional democracies
I'll trade you. Take my US passport and I'll take yours to live my life out in the EU.

62472269
>>62471442
lmao
healthcare is literally more expensive here unless you earn less than €24k or so ie you might as well not even work

62472277
>>62471442
If you actually believe Europe is better than the US, then GTFO of our country, instead of trying to destroy it with your socialist faggotry.

62472559
>>62470461
Jack ma did it

62473195
>>62470461
$100k is like 70th percentile here so it's not that common here either

62473217
>>62472277
No comrade, the whole world be turn to communism and worship niggers!

62473219
>>62473217
Nooooooooo!!!!!

62473247
>>62470461
Not really, im a europoor and in my sector its entirely possible to achieve 100k+

You know how much that is after taxes though? 58k

62473356
>>62472269
What the hell country are you from where healthcare is more expensive than the US? Every article comparing costs that I could find says they pay more than anyone else in the world, by a lot, and for questionable service even. Sure you might prepay in taxes, but even that is less than Amerifats pay for insurance, not even counting what they pay the hospital after insurance.

62473366
>>62471418
Lmao this is such cope, anyone in a decent non retail job has their healthcare covered, my job fully covers healthcare expenses over $3k per year, which I have never hit but it’s nice to have, I definitely pay way less than I would if I had to pay for it in taxes.

62474451
>>62473356
talk to a european you know isn't a total loser neet about what they pay in deductions and compare it to what you pay
i barely walk away with half my paycheck and over 10% of it goes to healthcare
do you or any american you know pay 10% of your Income to healthcare? i have never met one who does
even ones paying for personal cover only pay ~$400 a month and the premium is rarely relevant but even at $2000 it wouldn't be 10%
>and for questionable service even
american healthcare is some of the best in the world after countries like switzerland, japan, and norway
moreover they get something
i do not get any screenings or any kind of annual check up until i'm 45 minimum, unless i have a seizure etc
that is really what i hate, i get nothing beyond vague promises which wont come true because preventative medicine > curative medicine
it's like social security, the young are just paying for the old but it will never be reciprocated

62474728
>>62470461
I make $118k ($73k after taxes) a year working as a software engineer, non-US. I live with my parents and save/invest almost $5k a month. I also have a company match and I have a shitton of money in my retirement fund.
I must say I'm from one of the few countries where a software engineer still good money.
>>62471423
No they don't.

62474932
>>62470461
I make $156k CAD, but in a few more months that will be less than $100k USD so you're probably right.

62475517
>>62470461
>it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen
Start your own company. You can make as much as you want, if it's successful.

62477272
>>62474728
Israeli?

item.title: >sitting at my WFH job, watching Clavicular clips >go for an hour long walk at lunch time It's fucking insane that normi… — 18 replies — 44.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>sitting at my WFH job, watching Clavicular clips >go for an hour long walk at lunch time It's fucking insane that normi…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-13 19:55 UTC

OP

>sitting at my WFH job, watching Clavicular clips
>go for an hour long walk at lunch time

It's fucking insane that normies expect me to put the slightest shred of effort in to a job when I'm subhuman.

Early replies (first 3)

62476268
>>62476206
All wagies are cucks because they break their backs while male pornstars and chads have sex with milfs all day.

Quiet quitting is the new meta: why should I, an incel, work when others are paid to fuck? Burn it all to the fucking ground.

62476343
>>62476268
based

62476346
>>62476206
>duuuuuuude why isn’t my goyslop still whimsical!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476380
>>62476268
Funny what porn does to a man’s mind.

62476397
>>62476380
Who gives a fuck. You seem like someone that's OK with waging while other men fuck. Tell me how that is not more cucked.

62476401
>>62476397
There’s more to life than sex.

62476405
>>62476380
True. I’m incapable of any kind of a relationship with a woman bc I all I see/think about is fucking her. Ironically this makes me way more awkward and insecure bc I want to fuck her and there’s no quicker way on earth to give a woman the ick than to be awkwardly sexually attracted to her. I need to detach myself from the desire of lust. Step one is to stop looking at porn and stop jacking off.

It’s funny bc back in elementary school before I learned that I wanted to fuck girls, but still liked girls, I was able to easily get every girl I liked to like me back simply because I was just treating them like someone I wanted to be good friends with.

62476406
>>62476268
we both spend 8 hours a day on a computer at home. the difference is that i get paid $155k a year to do so. the other difference is that i go outside once i hit 8 hours.

62476412
>>62476406
Inb4 seething replies

62476576
>>62476401
Saying that to an incel is psychopathic. Fucky you, normie.

62476581
>>62476406
And I hope you get beaten by niggers the moment you step outside your door.

62476590
>>62476405
I hope there is a cure to this curse.

62476627
>>62476405
As an incel, it's always a mind fuck to me.
Act uninterested? 95% of the time they simply ignore you as well or 5% of the time you end up friend zoned if you have proximity (At work, friend of a friend, etc).
Act interested? Eww ICK, too forward, pervert, rejection, "I don't just want one thing!", etc.

62476708
>>62476627
The hint it to just be attractive

62476722
>>62476581
why are you so mad?

62477265
>>62476722
Why were you born a cuck

62477277
>>62476406
What is your job?

62477284
>>62476268
Sage

item.title: the betrayal was real — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
the betrayal was real

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:18 UTC

OP

This man has financially raped me over the last 6 years. I might have to get a job if LINK doesn't pump to triple digits by 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62475075
>>62475029
The betrayal was real indeed, but it came from bitcoin

62475079
>>62475075
not buying your russian jeetcoin, negro

62475085
He never promised you anything. You agreed to buy the token to fund his corporation, with no promise of any returns.

Dont blame him for the story you made up inside your own head

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475093
>>62475085
I was promised an Ethereum style bullrun back in 2018

62475097
>>62475093
Not by Sergey. By other people who made up stories inside their own heads

62475116
>>62475085
He promised becoming the dominant oracle and the cornerstone of crypto adoption.
He delivered this in spades.

He also promised the exclusive use of the token for node payments.
He also delivered this.

62476551
>>62475075
You are low IQ or deep-coping. The market is downstream macro and global liquidity events exogenous of crypto which feed into it but you are a RETARD if you think pricing mechanics don't change functionally, fundamentally on the basis of real utility being allowed and used. We are not always going to be a random speculative niche, and XRP specifically is well situated for this. It doesn't mean clarity will send the price up the second it is signed but it will absolutely be a necessary doorway to the kinds of change we need to have real systems migrate so they don't get fucked in a future admin agency guidance and fed direction etc. and the fact it is happening already has banks preparing so they are not late to the ones that do. You have a point very very narrowly but are wrong in the grander sense and in the specific sense about price not being affected by news as described by the two things mentioned even using your own example of the lawsuit both at the launch and resolution of it. You are blackpilled by uncritical impatience and it is affecting your analysis.

62476961
>>62475085
Yeah, Sergey was very careful to not shill the token ever. His project has been and will be successful without it. Fuck all the autistic retards who thought they were smarter than "normies" and that they would be rich by buying some stupid unknown magic coin without ever touching grass. Fucking hate these board spammers who still dont move on.

62477071
>>62475029
>triple digits
sir, I have bad news, the pool was redeemed

62477420
>>62476961
staking = shilling
they called the yield "rewards" for this reason, as a thin legal veneer. it is, however, now a security

>A-anon you're not going to sell right before the boomers will be forced to send SPCX to $600 r-right?

62476896
Wish I could pay $100 to gookmoot so he can upload a .wav file to the board for the retards who tried to gaslight me into thinking this was an obvious mooner

Going through my old threads now and o i am laffin! Still short and strong! Thanks baggies!

62476919

62477226
>>62476797
Their top valuation was equal to more than 30000 dólares estadounidenses to be extracted out of every working American.
Will the Americans allow their masters to do that to them? I think so. But I do not bet on it.

>>62476829
That is a year's net in Germany, after rent, migration donations and climate contribution. Please show some respect.

62477251

62477268

item.title: SPACE X STOCK.... don't hear much about that anymore, do you? — 11 replies — 25.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPACE X STOCK.... don't hear much about that anymore, do you?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-13 19:53 UTC

OP

SPACE X STOCK.... don't hear much about that anymore, do you?

Early replies (first 3)

62475586
spcx...whateva happened to that?

62475970
>>62475586
$600 and an $8T valuation end of week, says Morgan Stanley.

62475985
Still holding on to my early access SPCE shares, doesn't look like they got converted over yet

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475995
>>62475580
typical white trash pump and dump

62476009
it has a market cap of almost 2 trillion dollars. absolutely fucking retarded. This shit needs to drop 90% before it even remotely makes sense

62476288
>>62475580
Im not in spcx but this is just fund managers shorting to get a better price in rebalancing their portfolios, once they cover their allocation and balance their funds it'll go back up when they close their puts, but I doubt to the $300-$600 range GS is saying. It explains the sell off of tech these last few weeks.

62476351
>>62475586
I came here to post this.

62476373
>>62475580
Like silver and xrp

62476620
>>62476288
Kek nice try stupid baggie, we know you're all in the way in on Elons dick.

62476767
>OY VEY
20% of locked shares become eligible for sale after second-quarter earnings (scheduled: August 6)
Another early release could occur if the stock meets a price-based performance trigger (presumably, it won't)
From late August through October, additional tranches are scheduled to unlock every few weeks. The largest release comes after third-quarter earnings (early to mid-November), followed by the expiration of the six-month lockup in December.
It is estimated that insiders could be free to sell as much as 44% of the company by early September, increasing the tradable float by roughly 900% from IPO levels.

62477271
>>62476620
Nope. I stay out of IPOs they usally always have an initial rise and fast correction as insides cash in, but insiders havent even sold, i dont even think they can right now, so this market activity are from fund managers shorting to shake people out so they can get in at lower prices and rebalance their portfolios.

item.title: Do you ever think how you'd have handled if you have bought many bitcoins when it released and held until 2025? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you ever think how you'd have handled if you have bought many bitcoins when it released and held until 2025?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:16 UTC

OP

Do you ever think how you'd have handled if you have bought many bitcoins when it released and held until 2025?
Considering scams, MTGOX, and cashing out without people spying on your transactions?

Early replies (first 3)

62475977
>>62475888
I already know how I would have handled it because of how I have handled it.

I wasn't able to get in until 2014 because the shithole country I was living in didn't have any way of doing it, other than to buy your own computer and mine it at home, and I was literally penniless from 2009-2013, as in "sleeping on a couch because I got wiped out completely in the stock market" penniless. The only reason I didn't starve is because my mother allowed me to buy food on her credit card, but nothing else. So, no fancy computer and mining for me.

In 2014 when someone opened a Bitcoin ATM in my little shithole city, and I actually had spare change, I bought every penny's worth that I could afford. And I sat on it until it 160X'ed.

If I'd been able to obtain anything in 2010 I would have done the same and been fucking fabulously wealthy now. Even 100BTC would be completely life-changing.

62475988
>>62475888
>>62475977
I bought at $16k and sold at $24k.

If I bought from the very beginning I would've held it until I had $10 million and sold it all right then. I didn't even know about Bitcoin until like 2017 or something.

62476334
>>62475888
I held from 2016 till 2025.
Even still I havent sold it all and never will.

If anything BTC has taught me; Patience is the virtue to Greed's sin.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476361
I oft think about this, I would have legit fallen for a scam.

62477310
>>62475888
If i bought at $1 and held until 2021 pretty sure the feds would have killed me and steal my bitcorns. They wouldn't let a goyfag like me make it.
t. Bought a coin at 19k sold at 112k, the kabal allowed it

62477413
Its one of those things you think about

item.title: What stops you from building and selling e-commerce (not Amazon FBA) businesses like picrel? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What stops you from building and selling e-commerce (not Amazon FBA) businesses like picrel?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:08 UTC

OP

What stops you from building and selling e-commerce (not Amazon FBA) businesses like picrel?

Early replies (first 3)

62477258
Money that could be better spent on gambling.

62477280
>>62477203
>buy one of these businesses
>the easy auto pilot passive income monthly revenue mysteriously drops to 0

62477281
>>62477203
Google.
Thousands did this in the early 2000s.
They just pulled the rug on rankings for everyone.
T. 3000 Google clicks a day in 2008 and 300 a day now even though I've generally grown all around and the business is well known.

You only get to play if allowed to play in e-commerce these days.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477286
>>62477281
don't these businesses all advertise on social media (not Google SEO)?

62477328
>>62477286
The money it costs to advertise is prohibitory.

Ad rates are insider gigs.
If you aren't in the club you are paying 10x ppc relative to competitors.
I am on vacation with just a few minutes here or I'd write a book.
100% rigged in every possible regard.
Ads are priced (to non insiders) to entirely eliminate any reasonable margin.

I'm only around because I by built 1000 wholesale customers and a 50k pruned mailing list AHEAD of them shutting it down.

My top Google term, that leads to 80%+ of my search volume is literally my business name.
You can't imagine the level and extent of online marketplace and search rigging.

62477381
>>62477328
Hmm didn't realize this was rigged. I ran an ecom store before and also found my ads to be insanely expensive. I was making bits of money at first, then the ads increased so much in price that I couldn't justify keeping it going and had a ton of inventory I just decided to throw away. It was like a rugpull. I figured maybe I screwed up something else though. I tried going only organic traffic too, but it was so time consuming it was like working less than min wage for the effort.

item.title: >These guys aren’t making high art or anything. — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>These guys aren’t making high art or anything.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:13 UTC

OP

>These guys aren’t making high art or anything. They’re consuming every video they can, making cope 4chan threads, and jerking off all day. I’ve seen enough neet threads to know this is the case for 99%. If one is some high IQ scientific genius like Tesla or Einstein that’s different but for anyone below 130 IQ, it’s going to be a waste of time you’d get far fulfillment just working an actual career and building a family with some hobbies mixed in.

Yes, an average salary of $65k in most states is very productive. Working 9-5 white collar at best is so fulfilling.
Can we get a wagie cope thread?

Early replies (first 3)

62476871
>THE CONSPIRATORS IN MY HEAD JUST CONTROL EVERYTHING
sick and tired of this narrative.

62476887
>>62476871
Well, the wealth gap does keep growing. Whether someone controls it or not, that is what is happening.
It means things keep getting more expensive and people can’t keep up.

62476991
>>62476871
K shaped economy.
Whether by design or not, that is what is happening.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477130
>>62476871
>who is Epstein
He was just a frontman too kek. You probably still believe in voting too

62477405
>>62476871
you are like little baby

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 116 replies — 164.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 116 • Updated: 2026-07-13 19:37 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62471956
>>62468785
>If inflationary rewards "disassociate price from network usage" then that's true for pretty much all cryptos that have ever existed.
>Starting with the first one to ever exist.
Why don't you stop conjuring words I never said to argue against and address my point?

None of this is fooling anyone.

62471975
>>62466449
That is great and all but how will this affect the price of the Link token?

62472062
>>62471956
lol

That's literally what you said though:
>>62466708
>But link that is used to pay nodes doesn't come from the market. Which has been the main problem of why link is disassociated from the network.

>>62471975
See >>62466927

62472069
>>62466457
This will keep happening. Link won't re-enter the top 5 until btc goes to 35k

62472415
>>62472062
I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin. New Link that enters circulation comes not only from node operators selling but Chainlink Labs themselves selling tokens. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). Stop being dishonest about the Link token.

62472614
>>62472415
>miners decide the price of BTC
You're legitimately retarded.

62472624
>>62472415
>CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token
If users don't have to "buy" the Link token, they still have to "pay for" the token.
Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.

62472630
>>62472062
>>62466927
>Some anon did the math in another thread;
Link to thread?

62472819
>>62467960
Obviously I meant for us to track it

62472871
>>62472614
Point exactly what I said about BTC and plug both it and your reinterpretation into ChatGPT.

>>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC
Here's exactly what you said: "all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it"

You're a fucking retard who thinks miners determine the price of BTC.

>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
Holy shit yes they are.

62473105
>>62472871
>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
yeah this is definitely wrong lmao

62473264
>>62473105
That's exactly how all crypto inflationary rewards work.
For example: 95% of Bitcoin mining rewards are inflationary, and only like 5% have to be bought by users from the open market.

And in the case of Link, much of it is being bought on the open market via payment abstraction.

You cripples really need to learn how to sing a new tune.

62473278
>>62473264
No wonder you get grifted.
You dont understand supply and demand, how price is determined and that btc "inflation" =/= 8% supply increase aka "strategic unlocks"

62473280
>>62473278
No, you don't understand those things.

62474448
>>62472938
And what did I say after that?
>>62473105
>Holy shit yes they are.
>yeah this is definitely wrong lmao
What, the $1M a week Reserve buyback? The CCIP fee tokens stuck in a contract that have yet to be converted to Link? Realize when you give these fucking bullshit "technically..." answers, if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit. Why Chainlink is realizing the goals it was aiming to achieve for over 10 years in the release of DTCC's and SWIFT's DLT platforms and yet the price is $8. Why Chainlink is onboarding over $7B in protocol value to CCIP and the price is still $8. Hundreds of millions of dollars of revenue generated and still $8.

And notice you never directly addressed my points about $LINK being decoupled from the network. You keep making inaccurate comparisons.

62474875
>>62474448
>And what did I say after that?
After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

You're such a massive retard lmao.

>if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit
Two reasons:
1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
2) this >>62466457 >>62467657 >>62468805

62474886
>>62467048
whats stopping them from being paid in link then immediately selling that link for something else

62474889
>>62474886
lmao what's stopping literally any crypto miner/node on any L1 from doing that?

Why do link fuddies always have to be so explicit about being absolutely clueless on crypto?

62474913
>>62474448
well, i'll give you this pal, i did read, but i am, in fact, never selling.

62476934
>>62474889
because mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it, or revenue share or buybacks.

62476944
>>62476934
>mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it
please tell me you're only joking

62477003
This idea that link/xrp cucks have that banks will all of a sudden start buying mass amount of their respective shitcoins is truly a fascinating mass psychoses to behold. This board is basically an insane asylum at this point between the xrp and link spam.

62477008
>>62476944
the market must be a comedian too because that joke is told in every link pair chart

62477021
>>62477003
If banks want to use Chainlink (and they do), they have to pay for the work Chainlink nodes do, which is denominated and paid exclusively in Link tokens.

62477094
>>62474875
>After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
>Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

Anon, you're calling me a retard for saying a miner, someone running operations would sell bitcoins at a loss. What happens to a business that doesn't make a profit? What do you think goes into a the mind of a miner received BTC out of the coin base and is about it sell it. You think he's gonna say "hmm, I rely on this to keep my operations running, I should sell it for lower than what it takes to keep the lights running"?

>>62474875
>1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
But Chainlink's revenue is far from insignificant and they gloat about generating hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue generated. Link has always been suppressed. However, can you show that $Link has been battling it out and contributing to fighting against the suppression in contributing to its price? For reasons I previously mentioned multiple times that you refuse to directly address, CLL is sabotaging its own internet funny money by giving customers like myself an avenue to bypass it and pay in USD. And only a meager and unknown percentage has been traceably converted to Link between the Reserve, SVR, and CCIP.

>>62474913
no conflicts between not selling and being realistic about the current tokenonmics till staking releases.

62477138
>>62477021
Its a flat fee in dollars, converted to link, paid to node operators, sold for dollars. Its an unneeded hop.

62477152
>>62477094
You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

>>62477138
>Its an unneeded hop
It really isn't. For the simplicity and uniformity of the basic infrastructure alone it makes perfect sense to use a single token.
And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik admits the economic (dis)incentive aspect makes a token needed as well.

62477191
>>62477152
>And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik
He is jelly , he fiddled with the whole proof of work thing while he could have printed out of thin air and just dumped annualy in the form of token unlocks.

The only good thing with the link conversion/sale is the node operators can sell it for thei local jeetcoin currency.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 200 replies — 99.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 200 • Updated: 2026-07-13 18:19 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62473146
>>62468936
If you have a master's there should already be some bs job that hires you.
I know 2 people in your shoes and they did exactly that, some bs gov job or companies who contract with military.
The Master degree tells them you're not retarded and can learn the job but has no relevance to your schooling

62473155
>>62449961
I bet you were one of those neet posters and now mommy and daddy are getting to old or are dead/dying and you suddenly have to get that wagie job you were making fun of

62473189
>>62473155
>t. wagie
Kek. I am neet and also 40

62473197
>>62473155
Seething wagie, i live toyally of NEETbux + crypto. Tick tock, wagefuck.

62473199
>>62473155
Being a slave isn't a good thing. You're supposed to get out of a bad situation not build a personality around it and defend like a vegan feminist.

62473272
>>62473197
You the VA guy?

62473749
>>62473272
It’s the VA guy

62473786
Well, nevermind that shit. I just went to that store and saw who they hired instead of me. I don't know why I bothered.

62473888
It's the same shit as before, nobody even looks at my applications, nobody even talks to me.

62474038
>>62473888
you decided to look for jobs at the worst time, you retarded nigger

62474334
What are you supposed to do? Nothing you're told to do works.

62474529
>>62449961
just go sell cars. just go into sales. doesnt matter what

62474626
>>62451403
im in the same boat as you OP. start using your local resources. you said you live in the woods? start selling firewood. grow a bunch of plants and start a nursery and start selling plants. designer plants for decoration like japanese maples and shit. its a good side gig. make a website and shit for it then in a year or two when you apply for a real job say you were an entreprenuer and sold your business and want the security net of a regular 9-5 job now with the shitty economy. you can also just backdate this shit and fabricate a website and reviews for something that never happened too btw if you want to just start applying now withou getting any extra money or learning how business works

62474732
>>62450440
They don’t hire anyone retard

62474735
>>62452288
They
Don’t
Hire
You

62474751
even though I'm not a neet after being laid off I feel like I'm in a similar situation.
I have a leg up with my work experience and education but I don't feel like it was worth it which meant that I wasted my youth doing something even worse than just fucking around

62474795
to all the people in this situation who want actual sane advice, here it:

Do something easy. Something that won't demand a superior work ethic or a miserable grind with a manager looking at production numbers watching you.
You're likely gonna hate your life every single morning you wake up and have to work AND you didn't do this when you were 20 years old and it was easy. You can get over the hump and become a "worker" but the mornings are already your enemy, don't stack the odds further against you.

Work behind a register. Ideally at a gas station or some sleepy business. Maybe a long shot for you to get hired, but it's ideal. Some cleaning, some dealing with dickheads, some stocking. You won't be worked to the bone. A grocery/dollar store cashier is worse in most cases.

Inbound call centre. Yes you will hate your job but the work comes at you and your office should be air conditioned. If you have superior (compared to your coworkers) manners and people skills you may even find yourself promoted rapidly. 60 year old grannies get jobs in call centers and end up trainers and managers, you can do it.

Janitor. This one may be a stretch for a 40yo NEET but if you get the right gig, or hop into the right gig after getting a year experience, you may have found a cozy job. Many janitors are not running around like rats and barely have to deal with coworkers or the public. Maybe you end up hired by the local school board in half a year and make "decent" money.


If you really want to try your hand at job where you have a taskmaster watching you try these:

Warehouse worker.
Meatpacker.
Day laborer: Construction, landscaping, whatever.
Factory as a production worker. GL getting hired unless the job is literal hell with high turnover.
Outbound Call Centre

Good luck. If you show up every day for a 1 year or 2 a manager or senior coworker will likely give you a glowing reference. You can even become a real "worker" and have easier mornings in time.

62474821
>>62474795
None of those are hiring.

62474824
>>62474821
Then keep whining here like a bitch. Someone else will take the advice and make a few dollars. If a call centre won't take you, you may as well start picking up cans and bottles.

62474838
>>62474824
Just admit that you're wrong. There are no jobs.

62474844
>>62474795
if you're a man you don't exist. don't go to die in a dead end gas station. karma is real.

62474884
>>62449961
Anon, try asking an LLM (I recommend chat gpt but use whatever) on how to get a job. Explain your entire situation in as much detail as possible, why you haven't worked, depression, you're 40, never had a job, where you live (so it can recommend options) that you're carless.

Explain EVERYTHING. Learning how to utilize LLMs will really help you anyway. I'm getting close to 40 myself and I use LLMs daily (I am a professional with a good job not a NEET but it doesn't matter you need to use LLMs now even if you're employed). Anons ITT are mostly going to dunk on you because you're a huge fucking loser but the best time to turn your life around is right the fuck now. The LLM won't make you feel bad like everyone here is doing too.

Explain your interests to it too and maybe it can try and recommend a job you'll actually excel somewhat in, although beggars can't be choosy at this point.

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048
>>62476030
Nowhere did I say it's a crutch, I very clearly said it's a tool. You're starting to sound like you're beyond helping desu. Good luck.

62476501
>>62453688
The fact that you think there is any significant amount of “attacks” in this thread and not genuine criticism shows how far removed you are from the world. It’s going to be a tough couple years for you as you enter the workforce. You’ve received tons of honest advice.

62476747
>>62457849
Anon can we talk on private? I think I can help you with this job for a fraction of 5% of what you get paid. I am desperately in need of money right now.

item.title: Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? — 37 replies — 74.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26?

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-13 19:50 UTC

OP

Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? I used to be a NEET and then a wagie at a supermarket.

Early replies (first 3)

62475219
>>62475211
Dnr but would pump many a white baby out of that semen demen wemen

62475234
College is great for hot chick, tinder is a gooner dump site.

62475248
>>62475211
I heard that if you are in the US you can get into some accounting positions
with just a cpa loicense which you get through some expedited programs but Im not sure.
Look up some Clarey's vids he has a hard on for accounting and has always recommended it.

If you can get in without a degree than you should do that, you can get a degree on the side
through online programs while you are actually working.

If you have to get a degree than again - study online, don't waste time and money going to a brick and mortar
place it aint worth it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475315
>>62475306
People said the same shit when computers were invented, instead it made even more accounting jobs. The eternal accountant will never die.

62475380
You’re not even old and you’re overthinking it. You're going to have a huge advantage over these retarded little 18 year old Gen Alpha dudes when it comes to getting pussy. You’re more developed and you have more life experience, hoes like that shit. Start lifting hard if you aren’t already.

62475382
I remember being 18 in college and talking to a guy who went to the military before going to college and me and all of my friends were making fun of him for being 27 haha

62475396
>>62475382
The avg grad student is in his early 30s now. Kids are going to school later then in the past.

62475410
>>62475211
Claude fable will decimate accounting job market. Retard

62475424
>>62475410
What’s the safest major in the age of AI sloppa?

62475427
>>62475410
i'm a senior manager at a fortune 10 company. we recently had an AI demo week, showcasing claude and other tools. they all sucked and were a complete joke. what do you do for a living?

62475451
>>62475211
At this point pick up trades and become an electrician. Decent pay and assured source of income for decades.
>>62475427
This sounds like the people that laughted at genAI back in 2023 because it didnt know how to draw hands. It's shortsighted, and while it may very well plateau, just the possibility of it improving exponentially warrants some concerns, specially considering OP is deciding his professional trajectory for the next 35-40 years or so.

62475468
>>62475451
you're assuming that LLMs will overcome their inherent limitations concerning hallucinations or numerical calculation ability.

if all white collar work is at risk of LLM destruction (it's not), then why would anyone pursue a career as an electrician? who would be paying for your services as an electrician?

62475494
>>62475211
Learn the phrase "the combo or just the sandwich?"
Remember that no one asks "would you like fries with that?" ever since the combo meal deal was introduced.

Between accounting databases and AI, expect to be saying that phrase a lot after you graduate.

62475507
>>62475468
I am not assuming anything, I'm just saying that the POSSIBILITY of that technology being able to overcome such limits or at least improving itself enough to make OP field obsolete warrants him considering his options, not straight up discarding accounting.
About the electrician, it's a personal suggestion. Everything runs on electricity, houses will always need repairs, installations and maintenance of that kind, for example. If I could go back in time I would have picked that trade.

62475605
>>62475211
Wow she's cute, what an angle. nw needed to coordinate a careful approach towards her?

62475644
>>62475427
>>62475451
The thing about AI is it is useful, but even in its current iteration it has fundamental flaws that mean for specific jobs it can never do as good as an experienced human. Inexperienced people using AI will do better than if they didn't in many cases, but anybody with decent experience sees the output and sees all of the things wrong with it.
As someone put it, people think AI makes good charts or slides or reports, and then the people who are really good at making charts or slides or reports all say "No that looks bad".

I'm a software engineer and I use a decent amount of AI and had it do great for me, but there's still a number of problems I've thrown at it and it produced total unworkable trash. The thing is, if I was inexperienced I would think it looked good. As someone with 15 years of experience though, I could see its solution would break down rapidly with any other alterations we needed to make.
The experienced human in the loop is still required for anything beyond the absolute basic. As someone who is also in the financial space, AI is absolutely not going to be capable of keeping all the ducks of accounting and finance laws in a row versus an experienced human being.

62475661
>>62475644
best case scenario is AI performs like a retarded offshore indian "senior analyst" who actually performs at the level of a shitty onshore american staff accountant. the offshore indian costs $15k a year and, despite all of his defects, once you learn his personality you can kind of trust him, within limits. the AI will always be prone to confidently lying, it has no permanence or personality so you can never trust it, and the token spend on recreating an offshore indian IQ is $20k a year. so what's the point?

62475708
>>62475211
26 not that old. Older sure, but youre on the edge of being fine.
Just be cool. In all situations people will want to associate with you if you improve their life experience.
Also be serious & career oriented. Youre on the tail end of when fucking around is acceptable.

62475719
>>62475424
Healthcare and accounting

62475722
>>62475211

YOU

JUST

KNOW

62475737
>>62475719
the media is talking about the trades now
in about 10 years it'll be this

so you literally have the next 2-3 years to get your foot in the door in one of these fields where you can still last long enough to have a fulfilling career

62475745
>>62475722
It's almost guaranteed in the year 2026

62475849
>>62475719
>>62475737
Won't demand for healthcare decrease as the baby boomers die off? So if you're 5-10 years away from getting into medicine, you'll most likely enter the job market just as demand starts to take a heavy dip

62476033
>>62475849
we're not going to see a population decline and there is still a huge demand now for all healthcare roles. maybe less geriatric care and more plastics and specialist groups

62476056
>>62475315
That was because you still needed specialized knowledge. Now yiu just ask AI to use the tax codes for you.

62476101
>>62476056
an AI doesn't have the contextual knowledge to appropriately apply tax codes. it's similar to a layman looking up tax codes yet being fined and penalized by the IRS for misunderstanding and fucking things up.

62476748
>>62475719
with robotics and ai those two things are finished in 5-10 years.

62476768
Is it too late to do this at 35?

62476777
>>62475410
>dude just pay 500 dollars for 100 dollars of human labor

62477040
>>62476748
There aren't yet robots good enough to replace manual labor yet, and even besides that it still may require a revolution in energy technology. Your timeline is too immediate. Even for desk jobs, it will probably just mean the low performers lose their jobs and the high performers are expected to do more work (because of/with AI). Maybe models will improve to the point they can truly replace people completely, but I doubt it. They'll still need people to sign off on things as well because the AI companies would go bankrupt if they were held liable for the AI mistakes.

62477168
>>62475211
Are you fucking crazy?

62477217
>>62475234
Yeah..its so great wasting your time at school and studying and getting banged and then you graduate post-wall. lmao. Life is shit for females after 22.

62477250
>>62477217
Beats being an virgin incel in his late 20s like I am. Women can get men wanting them into their 40s and 50s. Men are lucky if one woman wants to have sex with them.

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 128 replies — 46.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 128 • Updated: 2026-07-13 17:35 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469665
this is some shit that a university student should be able to do as a minimum requirement. wtf is so complicated?

62469774
>>62468335
>Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average,
Seriously?? How many people here bought Chainlink or Safemoon? Not even mentioning all the Indian shitcoin scams.

62469777
>>62469347
>a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.
So what? How is that even relevant to the point that it was a Japanese scientist who invented the blue LED?

And the "investor" was the Japanese shit-tier semiconductor company he worked for, whose Japanese CEO saw the business deteriorating and threw the money at the Japanese scientist as a "hail Mary" attempt to create a product that would save the Japanese company.

"OMG he had an investor!!!" So fucking what?

62469825
>>62469777
>So fucking what?
None of this would happen without the $3 million investment. None of this. The guy was extremely lucky in this regard that he had such an opportunity.

62469856
it's using a net lmao

62469886
>>62466527
Did China steal blueprints from SpaceX?

62470354
>>62469886
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can get while still being a rocket that does propulsive landing. Cable catch, dual fuel, seven engined booster, closed cycle engines, oxidizer-rich preburner, etc.

62470360
>>62466535
Corporate espionage happens all the time regardless of its legality and there's zero chance that the Chinks aren't just being used by Western alphabet agencies. The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion. People do not share power, especially not over international borders.

>>62466548
Also this. You can generally assume that everything China ever says is just lies projected to a face-saving culture. Its for domestic consumption, not for us to speculate about.

62470370
Buy SPCX, the prices won’t get much lower than this. You don’t want to be an earth bagholder, do you?

62470418
>>62470360
>The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion

They should have done that before Chinese satellites ruined Trump's Iran adventure by providing Iran with the exact locations in real time of air defenses, aircraft parking spots and other assets in the region, as well as providing damage assessment so Iran didn't waste missiles on an already destroyed target

62470662
>>62466603
>The one really great thing about this is that maybe now China will stop crashing boosters onto their own citizens' villages.
It's due to the fact their spaceport is so far inland it's impossible to not drop on it's citizens. Russia's fall over Siberia, while USA shoots over oceans.

62471032
>>62466603
>>62470662
Rockets launched from Wenchang fall into the sea, not onto populated areas. It's the rockets launched from Xichang that fall onto populated areas. This is because the location for Xichang spaceport was chosen during the Cold War when security from military strike was considered very important. There are no unpopulated in the Chinese interior at the low latitudes from which you can launch to geosynchronous orbit. Xichang spaceport will probably be closed within 5 years, or at most 10 years.

62471086
>>62470662
only an issue because of hypergolic fuels
otherwise its just a random debris cloud

62471098
>>62466590
>regional conflict in eastern yurop
we're talking about big picture stuff here, anon. no one cares about the vatnik-gopnik war

62471197
>>62468297
>>62468335
>noo you HAVE to write in correct english and use common english saying correctly for us to trust you!
your country is owned by jews who speak your language only because it's convenient to them. the universal language of the future will be chinese, while the US will be speaking spanish in the next decades, so get used to it, RETARD.

62471338
>>62467458
>>62467520
so starship isn't a factor? the servers in space thing is not priced in?

62471395
>>62466527
Kek, of course it looks just like Elon's. If China didnt have the US to steal from theys be an absolute disaster failure state.

62471694
>>62471338
>the servers in space thing
Complete nonsense, zero chance of turning a profit, ever, without miraculous breakthroughs in materials science. I work as a cloud engineer in the space industry, I mange enormous numbers of servers that are used by an entire subdivision of our company that are employed for the sole task of determining if a satellite's design is going cause it to experience runaway heat issues and fail. The idea of putting racks of fucking GPUs of all things into LEO is what we would come up with if we wanted to create a parody of what a clueless pleb would try to pitch to investors. Its going to be another full self-driving mode, robotaxi, robovan (remember that one?), humanoid robots, and the mission to mars. Its a fantasy, its not happening. He'll probably try, no doubt, and it will work for a month or so, stock will pump massively, and then fail.

62471784
>>62469825
faggot, do you think nobody else would have invented the blue led?

62471789
>>62466535
what makes you think the US havent already been doing this for decades at this point in supposedly 'allied' western countries like japan and s korea? long before the chinese ever became a serious threat

62471902
>>62471694
>zero chance of turning a profit
anon, we are not talking about reality or about what YOU think about it. and investors think "oh, they made a reusable rocket, they surely have enough engineering knowledge or ingenuity to pull it off!", so people invest based on assumptions and fantasies rather than on reality. just look at the AI bubble...

62471927
>>62471784
He wouldn't. He wouldn't invent anything without the funding. You seem to be very angry about this.

62471932
>>62471927
who cares who does it, why do you think it matters and why do you think i care about that one guy?

62471938
>>62471932
Try to read the posts you're replying to you fucking retard

62471973
>>62471694
>turning a profit
why would you think this is important, bigger companies have survived for longer without profit, your small minded thinking does not work at this scale

62472725
>>62471395
>muh it's also a cylinder so it has to be a carbon copy
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can be. Different engine count, different propellants, different engine combustion cycle, different preburner, different recovery method.

62472738
>>62466624
>(Elon's never going to sell his half of the company.)
The fat arsehole has preferential stock.
You'd still need to suck his dick if you bought 80% of this fucking garbage.

62474107
spcx is gonna go down 20% at open because of this stupid FUD

62475125
spcx is going to 30 because they are about to float 400x the amount of shares lmao
u guys are complete retards

62476545
Current price: $138.58

Only like $3 over the IPO price and below the market open price.

This with most of the shares still locked up. I think I showed a chart and it should show that 60% of shares will be unlocked by October. That is a 12x increase in float. IMO this stock is either going down and trading sideways. If you like SpaceX, wait for a better entry point. Maybe around $90.
